nodeIntegration_enabled.impact: Renderer has full Node.js access — any XSS becomes immediate RCE via require('child_process')
nodeIntegration_enabled.note: Disabled by default since Electron 5. Finding this enabled is a strong signal.

contextIsolation_disabled.impact: Renderer JS can access preload script's scope — prototype pollution → RCE even without nodeIntegration
contextIsolation_disabled.note: Enabled by default since Electron 12. Disabling it re-opens a major attack surface.

webSecurity_disabled.impact: Same-origin policy disabled — renderer can load arbitrary remote content, make cross-origin requests

sandbox_disabled.impact: Renderer process runs without OS sandbox — compromised renderer has broader OS access
sandbox_disabled.note: Default changed to true in Electron 20.

allowRunningInsecureContent.impact: HTTPS page can load HTTP scripts — MITM can inject JS into the renderer

experimentalFeatures.impact: Enables Chromium experimental features — may expose additional attack surface

enableRemoteModule.impact: Remote module allows renderer to call main process objects — RCE path from renderer
enableRemoteModule.note: Deprecated and removed in Electron 14+. Finding it enabled suggests old/unmaintained code.

allowpopups_webview.impact: Webview can open new windows — navigation hijacking if content is untrusted

webviewTag_enabled.impact: Enables <webview> tag in renderer — webviews have their own security context that may differ from main window

no_will_navigate_handler.impact: Missing will-navigate handler — renderer can navigate to arbitrary URLs including file:// and javascript:
no_will_navigate_handler.note: Detected by checking that BrowserWindow has NO will-navigate listener registered

no_window_open_handler.impact: Missing setWindowOpenHandler — window.open() calls are not blocked or validated
no_window_open_handler.note: Detected by checking that BrowserWindow has NO setWindowOpenHandler
